slime.name: Slime
slime.lore: Loam-fat vermin of the Kingless Wood — the first mark on the board, and the reason your XP bar starts moving. Mara pays better if the gel hasn't leaked.

wolf.name: Wolf
wolf.lore: Kingless Wood pack-hunter; poacher bait and poacher competition. The packs den where the game trails cross, and they know a straggler when they smell one.

bandit.name: Greenhood Cutthroat
bandit.lore: Company muscle. The hood is dyed with wood-moss 'so the kings' creatures know who pays.' Brigands rob you; the Greenhoods robbed the kings — and paid them for the privilege.

skeleton.name: Skeleton
skeleton.lore: The restless dead — tithe-stock on the crypt stair at low ranks, Valdrenn's legion still holding the day-nine line at high ones. They are not at rest; they are in transit.

cacto.name: Cacto
cacto.lore: Sunscour ambush flora. It drinks what nothing else can find, and it is not above helping the finding along.

raptor.name: Raptor
raptor.lore: Sunscour surface pack-hunter. The pride tolerates raptors as beaters — and the Charter pays for fewer beaters.

minotaur_boss.name: The Gravelord
minotaur_boss.lore: The Pale King's warden-beast at the door between Greywatch's crypt and the galleries below: it keeps the living OUT and the tribute IN. The lower stair opens only over its body.

boar.name: Bristleback Boar
boar.lore: Kingless Wood game — legally some king's property, practically dinner. Every tusk sold in Greywatch is technically poaching. Mara doesn't ask.

giant_spider.name: Fen Weaver
giant_spider.lore: The broodmother's strays, strung wherever the fen-water creeps — the March's weavers are her outliers. Do not squeeze the sac.

bog_serpent.name: Bog Serpent
bog_serpent.lore: Gloomfen flood-fauna. The king's rising water brought them, and every year the water — and the serpents — reach a finger-width further into somebody's field.

mantrap.name: Strangler Bloom
mantrap.lore: The Elder Strangler's garden, wherever fen-water creeps. Each bloom is a cutting off the mother-rootstock — kill the gardener and the gardens go quiet, for a season.

elder_strangler.name: The Elder Strangler
elder_strangler.lore: The mother-rootstock of every strangler bloom in the march, grown through a drowned farmstead it wears like a shell. It does not chase; the fight is walking into the garden and killing the gardener at its heart.

lizardman.name: Fenblade Lizardman
lizardman.lore: Fen-folk who pay Grelmoss tribute in carrion and keep its dam patched. The fen's court, such as it is.

marsh_wisp.name: Marsh Wisp
marsh_wisp.lore: Corpse-candles over drowned farmland. They light the fen's hidden roads — follow them at your own judgment, and mind which color the light is.

ash_husk.name: Ash-Choked Husk
ash_husk.lore: The Furnace-King's dead workforce, still clocking shifts on the haul-roads of the Emberfells and the rift. The shift never ends; the dead never notice.

fire_elemental.name: Cinder Elemental
fire_elemental.lore: Sparks off the stoked rift — the Furnace-King's loose embers, drifting wherever the draft takes them.

bone_bat.name: Crypt Shrieker
bone_bat.lore: Ossuary vermin. It nests in the grading-halls and answers the Pale King's summons like everything else down there.

wraith.name: Vault Wraith
wraith.lore: Pale Court house-guard: tribute that graded 'fine.' It holds the cold vaults the way the sentinels hold Valdrenn — the office outliving the occupant.

ranks.lore: What a vault wraith becomes at the top of the world, where the tribute goes. It drifts the tribute-road like a toll that collects itself.
ranks.name: Waste-Shade

cinder_golem_boss.name: Furnace Golem
cinder_golem_boss.lore: The forge-court's master-construct — what the rift's fires are FOR, wearing armor. Whether it is the Furnace-King itself or merely wears the office, nobody has survived asking. The Foundry gate opens only over its coals.

lich_boss.name: Morvane the Hollow
lich_boss.lore: The Pale King. It taxes Greywatch in dead — the funerals are the tithe — and builds a court from the payments. Was it ever human? The bounty pays the same either way.

marauder.name: Ashpicker Marauder
marauder.lore: Ashpicker muscle picking Valdrenn's bones. Nothing in the capital was looted the day it fell — that came after, and this is who came.

gravehound.name: Gravehound
gravehound.lore: Battlefield feeders of the Sundering Fields. The hounds ate well here once and never left; the Barrow Alpha's packs still ring her mound.

fallen_soldier.name: Fallen Garrison Soldier
fallen_soldier.lore: Valdrenn's army, still holding the day-nine line at the mustering stones. The garrison never stopped marching; it only stopped being alive.

oathbound_sentinel.name: Oathbound Sentinel
oathbound_sentinel.lore: Vaelric's knights. The oath outlived the men, and the oath does not negotiate. Ser Osmund is their champion.

old_wallbreaker.name: Old Wallbreaker
old_wallbreaker.lore: The siege-beast driven against Valdrenn's gates, still yoked to its war-sledge forty years on, grazing among the men it killed. Too dumb to die, too huge to rot.

barrow_alpha.name: The Barrow Alpha
barrow_alpha.lore: The gravehound pack-mother, denned in a mass barrow of the war-dead. Her howl opens the kennels — silence her mid-cry and the mound stays quiet.

ser_osmund.name: Ser Osmund, the Gatekeeper
ser_osmund.lore: Champion of Valdrenn, holding the castle gatehouse against all comers forty years past the point of it. He cannot be argued past, only put down — and putting him down is a mercy.

sundered_king.name: Vaelric, the Sundered King
sundered_king.lore: The second dead king. He said NO to the tribute — once, for nine days — and what the First Tyrant left of him still holds court alone. His rally is a muster-call; the oath still answers.

greenhood_poacher.name: Greenhood Poacher
greenhood_poacher.lore: Company skirmisher — the green hood the whole company is named for. He shoots from cover and closes with a knife when the cover runs out.

powder_brigand.name: Powder Brigand
powder_brigand.lore: The company's powder-corps: Grole's people, lobbing flasks of what the sappers marched home with. Strafe — the lob leads nobody.

bandit_enforcer.name: Greenhood Enforcer
bandit_enforcer.lore: Company wall-of-meat. Toll collection with a face, and an iron bar to slow you down when you decline to pay.

hollow_cowl.name: Hollow Cowl
hollow_cowl.lore: The company's 'mystics.' Nobody in the company asks what's under the cowl either. It mends its kin mid-fight — hit it mid-cast and the mending never lands.

thrace_redcap.name: Thrace the Redcap
thrace_redcap.lore: Captain of the Greenhoods — a man playing king in the kingless wood, which is the joke and the tragedy. His cap is dyed the traditional way. The fort gate opens over his body.

quartermaster_grole.name: Quartermaster Grole
quartermaster_grole.lore: The company's real brains: Thrace holds the wood, Grole holds the LEDGER. His vault is shelved with tribute-grade goods addressed to creatures, and his fight is an audit. Nobody leaves owing.

camp_cur.name: Camp Cur
camp_cur.lore: Company tripwire-dogs, bred in the Run's kennels. Pull the dog, pull the camp.

stolen_goat.name: Stolen Goat
stolen_goat.lore: Somebody's tribute-herd, diverted. The company steals from EVERYONE. It wanders; then it bolts.

restless_bones.name: Restless Bones
restless_bones.lore: Fresh tithe-stock on the crypt stair — the dead in transit, not yet sorted. They are not at rest; nothing down here is.

ossuary_stitcher.name: Ossuary Stitcher
ossuary_stitcher.lore: Gallery artisan of the Pale Court. It grades the dead like cloth and sews the tribute into courtiers — the half-finished one on the master table is its work.

bone_warden.name: Bone Warden
bone_warden.lore: Foreman of the sorting-house. It shelves what the stitchers grade, and it wards the doors against the living.

ranks.lore: THE Bone Warden — the foreman at its own post, beside the down-shaft the tribute rides past seeing. The Court's door is attended while it stands.

grave_harrower.name: Grave Harrower
grave_harrower.lore: Gallery cull-officer. Its muster-call is a shift-bell: the stock rises, the shift forms, the culling resumes.

crypt_ghoul.name: Crypt Ghoul
crypt_ghoul.lore: Tithe-pilferer — it eats the stock. Even the Pale Court has vermin.

pallid_mourner.name: Pallid Mourner
pallid_mourner.lore: The grief the Court cannot process: a weeping nothing in an unswept chapel — one candle, benches knelt out of true. Do not touch it.

ranks.lore: What the weeping becomes when touched. Entering the chapel is the trap; the one candle should have been the warning.

ember_warplate.name: Ember Warplate
ember_warplate.lore: Foundry line-soldier — the weapon-tribute, armed with itself. Its captains hold the line-north floor of the works.

forge_tender.name: Forge-Tender
forge_tender.lore: Foundry maintenance-caste: it mends the line, and it mends the line-soldiers — mid-fight, if you let it.

frostplate_revenant.name: Frostplate Revenant
frostplate_revenant.lore: Frost armor in a fire land — a question every hunter asks. It stands its posts like an office, and the rime on the tribute-crates is its stamp.

ranks.lore: A collector off its post — unbound from whatever schedule it kept. The rift is not its home; the cold came down from somewhere.
The First Tyrant's tithe-collector at its true post, weighing what the kings send up. Kill one and the ledger is wrong at BOTH ends.
ranks.name: Tithe-Collector

slagback_troll.name: Slagback Troll
slagback_troll.lore: Emberfells ore-eater caste. It eats raw ore and vomits the slag the fells are terraced with — generations of pours, readable like tree rings.

old_kiln.name: The Old Kiln
old_kiln.lore: The Furnace-King's oldest servant — the first machine of the fells' production line, defending its feeding-adit. Kill the kiln and, for the first time in living memory, the fells stop smoking.

forge_ward.name: Forge-Ward
forge_ward.lore: Dormant Foundry sentries — the 'statues' at the doors that aren't statues. They wake when the line is threatened, or when something on the line calls.

forge_prototype.name: Forge Prototype
forge_prototype.lore: A construct off the Foundry's ascending line — each frame bigger than the last, and this one walks. Ask what the line is building toward.

ranks.lore: The prototype at the line's end, woken early — magnificent and incomplete, fighting with systems that visibly misfire. On the anvils behind it: the next one, half-built.
ranks.name: The Unfinished King

sandpicker.name: Sandpicker
sandpicker.lore: Ashpicker desert chapter. It robs Sekhat's tomb and sells the maps twice — once to the Charter, once to the Greenhoods.

withered_courtier.name: Withered Courtier
withered_courtier.lore: Sekhat's obedient court, still holding ledgers of tribute paid in full. Obedience killed the sands; the receipts survived.

duneshadow_lioness.name: Duneshadow Lioness
duneshadow_lioness.lore: Kaharat's pride — the king's huntresses. The herds Greywatch drives south every new moon end here. That's the arrangement.

kaharat.name: Kaharat, the Red Mane
kaharat.lore: The pride-king of the scoured surface — king of everything that still moves on the sand. Every herd the Council drives south feeds the pride: the arrangement, met on the hoof.

sekhat.name: Sekhat the Ninth
sekhat.lore: The first dead king. He paid EVERYTHING — grain, gold, herds, finally the river itself — and it saved nothing. He walled his court into a tomb still wearing his tribute-ledgers.

glimmereye.name: Glimmereye
glimmereye.lore: Harmless fen-light critter. Children of the marsh-wisps, or the wisps' bait. Unclear. It stays unclear.

fen_slime.name: Fen Slime
fen_slime.lore: Grelmoss's shed substance — the fen budding off its king. Where the water rises, so do they.

fen_slimeling.name: Fen Slimeling
fen_slimeling.lore: Buds of the buds. The fen is generous that way.

bloatslime.name: Bloatslime
bloatslime.lore: A fen slime that ate too well below the dam. What it has swallowed does not stay swallowed.

grelmoss.name: Grelmoss, the Crowned Mire
grelmoss.lore: The fen's king and the fen's dam: its crowned bulk sits in the outflow gorge, and the country behind it drowned in a generation. Water that leaves is tribute lost. Kill the dam, and somewhere north water MOVES.

aelthir.name: Aelthir, the Unmarred
aelthir.lore: The thing even kings avoid. It predates them — possibly predates everything — it initiates nothing, and it finishes everything. Mara will not buy the horn, and Mara will not say why.

cinder_nightmare.name: Cinder Nightmare
cinder_nightmare.lore: A war-horse still burning, forty years past its rider. The fires of the day the capital fell never quite went out.

ranks.lore: The king's own warhorse, circling the castle at a canter it has not broken in forty years. It is not looking for a rider. It is keeping formation.

sarquun.name: Sarquun, the Undertide
sarquun.lore: The thing that drank the sea. Grown too vast for its own pit, it surfaces on a schedule to feed — the countdown at the crater gate IS the feeding schedule. The desert above is its receipt.

pale_courser.name: Pale Courser
pale_courser.lore: A pale rider-beast of the high waste, patrolling the tribute-road it was left to keep. Nothing warm has crossed it in forty years, and it means to hold the record.

snow_harpy.name: Snow Harpy
snow_harpy.lore: Carrion-wise and patient, it works the tribute-road for whatever the cold finishes first. Up here, that is most things.

rime_warden.name: Rime Warden
rime_warden.lore: Paired guardians at the tribute-court's gate — statues that animate, posted where every payment must pass. They fight as the pair they were carved to be; splitting them is punished.

first_tyrant.name: The First Tyrant
first_tyrant.lore: The one even kings kneel to. It has no name — the board's top row reads only 'THE FIRST.' Its court is a tribute-yard: the whole world's payment, sorted and frozen, and its reinforcements arrive like deliveries. No one has ever felled it.
